title: moquiniastrum polymorphum subsp. ceanothifolium
intro: Moquiniastrum polymorphum subsp. ceanothifolium, also known as the "milk bush" or "canyon milkweed," is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, derived from its sap, which contains toxic cardenolides. The health benefits of Moquiniastrum polymorphum subsp. ceanothifolium include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant, often used in traditional medicine, include infusions, salves, and poultices, although caution is advised due to its toxicity.
